title: Clay Pigeons (ver 2)
body:
{comment: Intro}
[G]    [C]    [G]    [D] 
[G]    [C]    [G]  [D]  [G] 

{comment: Verse 1}
I'm goin' [G]down to the Greyhound Station, gonna buy a [C]ticket to ride
Gonna [G]find that lady with two or three kids and sit [D]down by her side
[G]Ride 'til the sun comes up and down around me 'bout [C]two or three times
Smokin' [G]cigarettes in the last seat
Sing my [D]song for the people I meet
And get [G]along with it all
where the [C]people say "y'all"
Sing a [G]song with a friend
Change the [D]shape that I'm in,
And get [G]back in the game,
And start [C]playin' again[G][D]

{comment: Verse 2}
I'd [G]like to stay but I might have to go to start [C]over again
Might [G]go back down to Texas, might go to somewhere that [D]I've never been
And [G]get up in the mornin' and go out at night
And I won't [C]have to go home
Get used to [G]bein' alone
Change the [D]words to this song
Start [G]singin' again[C][G][D]

{comment: Instrumental}
[G]    [C]    [G]    [D] 
[G]    [C]    [G]  [D]  [G] 

{comment: Verse 3}

I could [G]build me a castle of memories just to have [D]somewhere to go
Count the [G]days and the nights that it takes to get back in the [C]saddle again
Feed the [G]pigeons some clay
Turn the [D]night into day
Start [G]talkin' again, when I k[C]now what to say[G][D]

{comment: Instrumental}

title: Magnolia Wind
body:
Guy Clarke sings this in Eb, the John Prine/Emmylou Harris version is in Db, so 
transpose accordingly :)

{comment: Verse 1}
I'd rather sleep in a b[D]ox like a bum on the s[A]treet
Than a fine feather b[G]ed without your little ol' cold f[A]eet
And I'd rather be d[D]eaf, dumb, and stone[A] blind
Than to know that your m[G]ornings [A]  will never be m[D]ine

{comment: Verse 2}
And I'd rather die y[D]oung than to live without y[A]ou
And I'd rather go h[G]ungry than to eat lonesome s[A]tew
You know it's once in a l[D]ifetime and it won't come ag[A]ain
It's here and it's g[G]one [A]  on a magnolia w[D]ind

{comment: Chorus}
I'd rather not w[C]alk [G]  through the garden ag[D]ain
If I can't catch your s[A]cent on a magnolia w[D]ind

(Break)
  [D]        [A]        [G]        [A]       
  [D]        [A]        [G]   [A]     [D] 

{comment: Verse 3}
Well if it ever comes t[D]ime that it comes time to [A]go
Sis' pack up your f[G]iddle, Sis' pack up your [A]bow
If I can't dance with y[D]ou then I won't dance at a[A]ll
I'll just sit this one [G]out [A]  with my back to the w[D]all

{comment: Chorus}
I'd rather not h[C]ear [G]  pretty music ag[D]ain
If I can't catch your f[A]iddle on a magnolia w[D]ind

If I can't catch your s[A]cent on a magnolia w[D]ind

title: Make You Feel My Love (ver 3)
body:
Make You Feel My Love - Bob Dylan

Video Here - https://youtu.be/fdWto-AUM3Q

Capo on 1st fret

{comment: Verse 1}
[C]When the rain is blowing [G]in your face
[Bb]And the whole world is [F]on your case
[Fm]I could offer you a [C]warm embrace
[D9]To make you [G7]feel my [C]love

{comment: Verse 2}
[C]When evening shadows and the [G]stars appear
[Bb]And there is no one there to [F]dry your tears
[Fm]I could hold you for a [C]million years
[D9]To make you [G7]feel my [C]love

{comment: Bridge 1}
[F]I know you haven't made your [C]mind up yet
[E7]But I would [F]never do you [C]wrong
[F]I've known it from the moment [C]that we met
[D9]No doubt in my mind where you be[G]long

{comment: Verse 3}
[C]I'd go hungry, I'd go [G]black and blue
[Bb]I'd go crawling down the [F]avenue
[Fm]No there's nothing that I [C]wouldn't do
[D9]To make you [G7]feel my [C]love

{comment: Instrumental}
[C]      [G]     [Bb]     [F]    [Fm]      [C]    [D9]     [G7]      [C] 

{comment: Bridge 2}
[F]The storms are raging on the [C]rollin' sea
[E7]And on the [F]highway of re[C]gret
[F]The winds of change are blowing [C]wild and free
[D9]You ain't seen nothing like me [G7]yet

{comment: Verse 4}
[C]I could make you happy, make your [G]dreams come true
[Bb]Nothing that I [F]wouldn't do
[Fm]Go to the ends of the [C]earth for you
[D9]To make you [G7]feel my [C]love

{comment: Outro}
[C]      [G]     [Bb]     [F]    [Fm]      [C]    [D9]    ([G7]Fade)[C]

title: If I Should Fall Behind
body:
This is done from the version on the plugged album but it's the same thing.
Any comments email me at floordemon@hotmail.com

[D]          
[Bm]          
[G]          

[D]We said we'd walk together, baby, come what may
[D]That come the twilight, should we lose our [Bm]way
If as we're wal[G]king, your hand should sli[D]p free
I'll wait for y[Bm]ou
Should I f[G]all behind
[D]Wait for me

[D]We swore we'd travel, darling, side by side
We'd help each other, stay in [Bm]stride
But each lover's steps fa[G]ll so different[D]ly
But I'll wait for y[Bm]ou
And if I should f[G]all behind wait for [D]me

Now everyone dr[G]eams of a love lasting and t[D]rue
But you and [G]I know what this world can [Bm]do
So let's make our steps c[G]lear that the other may s[D]ee
And I'll wait for y[Bm]ou
If I should f[G]all behind
Wait for m[D]e

[D]Now there's a beautiful river in the valley ahead
There 'neath the oak's bough, soon we will be[Bm] wed
Should we lose each o[G]ther in the shadow of the evening's t[D]rees

title: Jesus Doesnt Want Me For A Sunbeam (ver 2)
Just Breathe (ver 2)
body:
If you fingerpick it, you will be missing a few notes, play around with your pinky - 
high E string on the 3rd fret then fifth fret for the C and the B string on the 
3rd fret for the G during the verses.

{comment: Intro}

[C]     [G]       

{comment: Verse 1}

[C]Yes, I understand that every life must end, uh[G]-huh
[C]As we sit alone, I know someday we must go, uh-[G]huh
Oh, [C]I'm a lucky man, to count on both hands the ones [G]I love
[C]Some folks just have one, yeah, others they got none, u[G]h-huh

{comment: Chorus}

[Am]  Stay with me...
[F]Let's just breathe...

{comment: Interlude}

[C]     [G]

{comment: Chorus}

[Am]  Stay with me
You're[F] all I see...

{comment: Bridge}

[G7]As I come clean...

{comment: Verse 3}

I [C]wonder every day, as I look upon your face, u[G]h-huh
[C]Everything you gave and nothing you would take, o[G]h no

[C]     [G]

title: Naked As A Window
body:
I'm not sure what key the song is in, but this works anyway.

How [C]hard for the [C/B]covers to be p[Am]ulled from their s[G]lumbers,
but a g[F]host never g[G]ives up its s[C]heet.
Tell me, is it the s[C/B]ame for the r[Am]attling c[G]hains or what[F]ever you w[G]ear when 
you s[C]leep?
You rem[F]ember the time that I told you to take off your c[C]lothes?
You were [C/B]naked as a w[Am]indow.
But I'll t[C]ake all that no[G]thing over n[F]othing at a[G]ll.[C]
I'm just a h[C/B]allway for c[Am]eilings and w[G]alls, babe
and [F]emptiness [G]all the way t[C]hrough.
You know you're [C]asking too [C/B]much to be [Am]held and not [G]touched,
but some[F]how that's j[G]ust what you [C]do.
You rem[F]ember the time that I told you to take off your c[C]lothes?
You were [C/B]naked as a [Am]window
But I'd ta[C]ke all that not[G]hing over no[F]thing at al[G]l.[C]
Yeah I'd t[C]ake all that no[G]thing over n[F]othing, over n[G]othing at [C]all.

title: Perfect
body:
Play: G

For chords in original key transpose this chord sheet +1 (and "use flats").

Play this song with the regular shapes of the chords given in this song
or use the ones that can be heard on the recording:

      E-A-D-G-B-e
G     3-x-0-0-3(3)
Em    0-2-2-0-3(3) (= Em7)
C     x-3-2-0-3(3) (= Cadd9)
{comment: Coda}
Dsus4 x-x-0-2-3-3 or x-x-0-0-3-3 (= a D chord which is neither major nor minor)
G/B   x-2-0-0-0-3  (= G chord with a "b" as the root)
D/F#  2-x-0-2-3-x  (= D chord with a "f#" as the root)

The "%" signs mean "repeat the bar before", so "| D | % |" = two bars of D. 
It's a common abbreviation. (Real Book etc.: https://www.swiss-jazz.ch/standards-jazz/AllOfMe.pdf)

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

body:
Sam Stone chords
John Prine  1971 (live version)  

https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Sam_Stone_(song)

Sam [D]Stone came home,
to his [G]wife and family,
after [A]serving in the conflict over[D]seas.

And the time that he served,
had [G]shattered all his nerves,
and [A]left a little shrapnel in his [D]knee. 
But the [G]morphine eased the pain,

and the grass grew round his brain,
and [E7]gave him all the confidence he [A]lacked;
with a [E]Purple Heart and a [E7]monkey on his [A]back.

There's a [D]hole in daddy's arm where all the [Em]money goes,
and [G]Jesus Christ died for nothin' I sup[A]pose.
Little [D]pitchers have big ears,
don't [Bm]stop to count the years,
sweet [E7]songs never last too [E]long on broken radi[A]os. [A]Mm[Asus4]m....[A][A]

Sam [D]Stone's welcome home,
didn't [G]last too long,
he [A]went to work when he'd spent his last [D]dime

And Sammy took to stealing,
when he [G]got that empty feeling,
for a [A]hundred dollar habit without over[D]time. 
And the [G]gold rolled through his veins,

like a thousand railroad trains,
and [E7]eased his mind in the hours that he [A]chose;
while the [E]kids ran around [E7]wearin' other peoples' [A]clothes...

There's a [D]hole in daddy's arm where all the [Em]money goes,
and [G]Jesus Christ died for nothin' I sup[A]pose.
Little [D]pitchers have big ears,
don't [Bm]stop to count the years,
sweet [E7]songs never last too [E]long on broken radi[A]os. [A]Mm[Asus4]m....[A][A]

Sam [D]Stone was alone,
when he [G]popped his last balloon,
[A]climbing walls while sitting in a [D]chair

Well, he played his last request,
while the [G]room smelled just like death,
with an [A]overdose hovering in the [D]air
But [G]life had lost its fun,

and there was nothing to be done,
but [E7]trade his house that he bought on the G, I. [A]Bill;
for a [E]flag draped casket on a [E7]local heroes' [A]hill

There's a [D]hole in daddy's arm where all the [Em]money goes,
and [G]Jesus Christ died for nothin' I sup[A]pose.
Little [D]pitchers have big ears,
don't [Bm]stop to count the years,

title: Bruised Orange
body:
#----------------------------------PLEASE NOTE--------------------------------#
#This file is the author's own work and represents their interpretation of the#
#song. You may only use this file for private study, scholarship, or research.#
#-----------------------------------------------------------------------------#

Date: Sun, 29 Mar 1998 19:22:16 -0500
From: Ray Allis <rallif@home.com>
Subject: CRD:p/prine_john/bruised_orange.crd

BRUISED ORANGE
by John Prine
interpreted by Ray Allis

   [G]My heart's in the ice house
2) I've been brought down to zero

   come hill or come valley
2) pulled out and put back there
   like a [C]long ago Sunday
2) I sat on a park bench

   when I walked through the alley
2) kissed the girl with the black hair
   on a [G]cold winters morning
2) and my head shouted down
   to the [D]church house
2) to my heart
   just to shovel some [G]snow
2) you better look out below

   I heard sirens on the train tracks
2) Well it ain't such a long drop

   howl naked , gettin nuder
2) don't stammer, don't studder
   an [C]alter boy had been hit
2) from the diamonds in the sidewalk

   by a local comuter
2) to the dirt in the gutter
   just from [G]walking with his back turned
2) you'll be carrying those bruises
   to a [D]train
2) to remind you
   that was coming so [G]slow[G7]
2) wherever you go

   (CHORUS)
   You can [C]gaze out the window

   get mad and get madder
   throw your [G]hands in the air

   and say what does it matter
   but it [D]don't do no good
   to get [C]angry
   so help me I [G]know[G7]
   for a [C]heart stained with anger

   grows weak and grows bitter
   you [G]become your own prisoner

   as you watch yourself sit there
   wrapped up in a [D]trap
   of your very [C]own
   chain of [G]sorrow

3) (Repeat first verse and chorus)

Please send corrections or suggestions.

title: Dont Think Twice Its All Right
Bob Dylans Beard
body:
This song is played on a 12-string guitar with a capo on the third fret 
Just noodle around the chords to hit the notes that jeff does for the melody

{comment: Intro}
[G]     [C]    [G]  [C]  [D]

title: Depreston
body:
This brand new song has been played by the Australian artist in some of her shows,
yet not recorded in a studio version. Enjoy!
Updated May 2018 from the studio version.

All of the song is played with two chords, C and F.

title: On And On And On
body:
Found some rough tabulature on this song and decided to work it out as it’s not been
anywhere except the viachicago forum.  This should be pretty close?

Piano intro:

e  ——0         -——0

B  —8—    x8   —8—    x8

G —11—         —11—

D —10—         —9—

If you’re a finger-picker you can alternate thumb, index, middle then ring (on the e) or
with the thumb.  (I think the brushing sounds better).  If you are a piano player, it's
F# G then E and B F# G then E.  (This riff may change but I generally don't carry it 
the beginning so... it's all yours to figure out).

Chords:

[CM7]                              

[Bm7]                                                 

[Am7]                    

[G]                            

[D]                  

I switch between these different voicings a lot so I figured I'd share them all  Do what
think sounds best.  I also play with a capo on 3, but that just fits my voice better,
are for original album tuning.  Either way, great song.  Hope someone finds this useful.

[CM7]On and on an[Bm7]d on we'll stay together yeah
[Am7]On and on an[G]d on we'll be together yeah
[CM7]You and I wi[Bm7]ll try to stay together yeah
[Am7]On and on an[G]d on we'll be together yeah

[CM7]Please[Bm7] don't cry we're designed to die
[Am7]Don't deny w[G]hat's inside
[CM7]On and on and [Bm7]on we'll stay together yeah
[Am7]On and on and on
[D]On and on and on

[CM7]One day we'll [Bm7]disappear together in a dream
[Am7]However shor[G]t or long our lives are going to be
[CM7]I will live in y[Bm7]ou or you will live in me
[Am7]Until we dis[G]appear together in a dream

[CM7]Please [Bm7]don't cry we're designed to die
[Am7]You can[G]'t deny even the gentlest tide
[CM7]On and on and [Bm7]on we'll be together yeah
[Am7]On and on and on
[D]On and on and on
We're go[CM7]ing to try

(Not sure about this instrumental, but I think it's mainly CM7, D and Am7 (if you're
this acoustically, it doesn't much matter))

[CM7]Please don't cry
This worl[Bm7]d of words and meanings makes you feel
outside
Something that you feel already
[Am7]deep inside
You've denied
[G]Go ahead and cry

[CM7]On and on an[Bm7]d on we'll stay together yeah
[Am7]On and on and on
On and on and on

[CM7]You and I w[Bm7]ill stay together yeah
[Am7]You and I wi[G]ll try to make it better yeah

Enjoy.  Post corrections or additions.

title: How Lucky
body:
This tab is made of a version of John playing this live from a DVD I rented years ago.
This is exactly how he played it that day. He plays it in a lot of different keys so,
have a go at moving the capo around to match the version you're used to.
Whether you choose to fingerpick or strum, these chords sound great and the song is a gem. 
More people should hear this one that's why I tabbed it out.

{comment: Verse 1}
[C]Today I walked down the street I use to [F]wander
Yeah, shook my [C]head and made myself a [G]bet
There was all these [C]things that I don't think I [F]remember
Hey, How [C]lucky can [G]one man [C]get.

{comment: Verse 2}
I bronzed my [C]shoes and hung from a rearview [F]mirror
Bronzed adm[C]iration in the blind spot of re[G]gret
There was all these [C]things that I don't think I [F]remember
Hey, How [C]lucky can [G]one man [C]get.

{comment: Verse 3}
Today I [C]walked down the street I use to [F]wander
Yeah, scratched my [C]head and lit my cigar[G]ette
Well, There was all these [C]things that I don't think I [F]remember
Hey, How [C]lucky can [G]one man [C]get.

{comment: Outro}
[F]Hey, How [C]lucky can [G]one man [C]get.
[F]Hey, How [C]lucky can    [G]One... Man... [C]Get.

title: Henrietta Indiana (ver 2)
body:
Artist: Josh Ritter
Song: Henrietta, Indiana (Live)
Album: Sermon on the Rocks
Standard tuning; Capo 3rd
(Josh's official songbook shows same chords with capo 5. Some other live recordings he uses capo 4. I suggest 
playing it as it suits you.)

From live acoustic recordings: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=2pQZRXm9gN8 and 
                               https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=BM0b3UoqYZg

Am/G: 302210
Am/F: 102200

Opening/Riff (x2)

e|--0--3--0-------------------|
B|--1--------3--1--0----------|
G|--2-----------------2p0-----|               
D|--2----------------------0--|              (He plays the open D string with his fret hand middle finger
A|--0-------------------------|                like the pull-off on the G string though it doesn't sound
E|--x-------------------------|               much different to just play it with the right hand like normal)

[Am]Henrietta, Indiana mill town
Locked th[C/G]e factory down and shut it up tight
My da[G]ddy and my brother and sixteen-hundred others
Lost e[F]verything they had in the night
[Am]Daddy got a taste for the hard stuff
He[C/G]nrietta, Indiana was dry
We'd[G] ride out to Putney, he'd tell me that he loved me
The [F]drive home was always so quiet
He had a devil in his [Dm]eye, [G]eye
Like a[Am] thorn in the paw
[Am/G]Disregard for the law
[F]Disappointment to the Lord on high

(Riff (x1))  Am

My [Am]brother practiced preaching in the basement
Perspiratio[C/G]n on his face 'til I knew
That s[G]omething was missing

His spirit was willing
He [F]could not believe it was true
[Am]"Blessed be the poor," he said
"Y[C/G]our treasure is on high."
All [G]of Henrietta, Indiana heard me hallelujah
When [F]I finally saw the devil in his eye
I saw a devil in his [Dm]eye, [G]eye
Like a[Am] thorn in the paw
[Am/G]Disregard for the law
[F]Disappointment to the lord on high

I wa[C/G]s coming home late from a midnight to eight
The r[G]adio said they'd ID'd the plates
Left [Am]three men dead, made their escape
By n[F]ow said the sheriff, they'll be in the next state
Will we[C/G] be able to catch them? Can they bring the dogs in?
Can th[G]ey call up the Bureau? Do they have next-of-kin?
Cameras[Am] came to our door and I opened it wide
They t[F]hought I was crying, it was something in my [Am]eye, [C/G]eye, e[G]ye,[F]
[Am]eye, [C/G]eye, ey[G]e, oh[F]

[Am]     [C/G]       [G]     [F] 
[Am]     [C/G]       [G]     [F] 

At n[Am]ight I leave a bottle on the table
The Bible [C/G]open to the Sermon on the Mount
Bless[G]ed be the poor of Henrietta, Indiana
But [F]happy are the ones that get out
I th[Am]ink I'll drive over to Putney
Th[C/G]e store'll be open to twelve
The [G]empty parking lot, the lights, the lonely kid, the register
I s[F]ee it all clear as a bell
I got a devil in my [Dm]eye, [G]eye
Li[Am]ke a thorn in the paw
[Am/G]Disregard for the law
[F]Disappointment to the Lord on high
[Am]Eye, [G]eye
[F]Like a thorn in the paw
[F]Disregard for the law

But [C/G]the wind blew me back [G]via Chicago

title: Thin Blue Flame
body:
{comment: Intro}
  [C]      [F]      [C]      [F]     

{comment: Chorus}
[C]  I became a thin blue fla[F]me
[C]  Polished on a mountain r[F]ange
[C]  And over hills and fields I [F]flew
[C]  Wrapped up in a royal [Am]blue

{comment: Verse 1}
I flew over Royal C[F]ity last night
A bullfighter on the horns of a n[C]ew moon’s light
Caesar’s ghost I saw[F] the war-time tides
The prince of Denmark’s fa[C]ther still and quiet
The whole world was looking [F]to get drowned
Trees were a fist shaking themse[C]lves at the clouds
I looked over curtains and it was t[F]hen that I knew
That only a full house gonna[C] make it through

{comment: Chorus}
[C]  I became a thin blue w[F]ire
[C]  That held the world above the f[F]ire
[C]  And so it was I saw beh[F]ind
[C]  Heaven’s just a thin[Am] blue line

{comment: Verse 2}
If God’s up there he&r[F]squo;s in a cold dark room
The heavenly host are just the [C]cold dark moons
He bent down and made the world in [F]seven days
And ever since he’s bee[C]n a’walkin' away
Mixing with nitrogen in lo[F]nely holes
Where neither seraphim or r[C]aindrops go
I see an old man wandering the ha[F]lls alone
But only a full house gonna m[C]ake a home[F]

{comment: Chorus}
[C]  But I became a thin blue [F]stream
[C]  The smoke between asleep and [F]dreams
[C]  And in that clear blue undert[F]ow
[C]  I saw Royal City far bel[Am]ow

{comment: Verse 3}
Borders soft with refuge[F]es
Streets a’swimming with[C] amputees
It’s a Bible or a bullet they put over[F] your heart
It’s getting harder and harder[C] to tell them apart
The days are nights and the ni[F]ghts are long
Beating hearts blossom into w[C]alking bombs
And those still looking in the clear blue sk[F]y for a sign
Get missiles from so high they might as wel[C]l be divine
Now the dogs are howling at our do[F]or
Singing bout vengeance like it’s [C]the joy of the Lord
Bringing justice to the enemies not the [F]other way round
They’re guilty when killed and th[C]ey’re killed where they’re found
If what’s loosed on earth wi[F]ll be loosed up on high
It’s a Hell of a Heaven we mus[C]t go to when we die
Where even Laurel begs Hardy for [F]vengeance please
The fat man is crying on his [C]hands and his knees
Back in the peacetime he caught [F]roses on the stage
Now he twists indecision takes b[C]ourbon for rage
Lead pellets peppering aluminu[Am]m
Halcyon, laudanum and Opiu[C]m
Sings kiss thee hardy this poisone[Am]d cup
His winding sheet is busy [C]winding up
In darkness he looks for the [Am]lights that have died
But you need faith for the same reasons that it&rsq[C]uo;s so hard to find
And this whole thing is headed for a[Am] terrible wreck
And like good tragedy that&rsq[G]uo;s what we expect

  [C]      [F]      [C]      [F]     
  [C]      [F]      [C]      [Am]     

{comment: Verse 4}
At night I make plans for a c[F]ity laid down
Like the hips of a girl on the s[C]pring covered ground
Spirals and capitals like the [F]twists of a script
Streets named for heroes that could [C]almost exist
The fruit trees of Eden and the ga[F]rdens that seem
To float like the smoke from the [C]lithium dream
Cedar trees growing in the co[F]ol of the squares
The young women walking in the p[C]ortals of prayer
And the future glass buildings and the p[F]ast an address
And the weddings in pollen and the wi[C]ne bottomless
And all wrongs forgotten and all [F]vengeance made right
The suffering verbs put to [C]sleep in the night
The future descending like a [Am]bright chandelier
And the world just beginning and the g[G]uests in good cheer
In Royal City I fell i[Am]nto a trance
Oh it’s hell to believe there ain[G]’t a hell of a chance

  [Am]      [G]      [Am]      [G]     
  [Am]      [G]      [Am]      [G]     
  [C]      [F]      [C]      [F]     

{comment: Chorus}
[C]  I woke beneath a clear blue s[F]ky
[C]  The sun a shout the breeze a [F]sigh
[C]  My old hometown and the streets I [F]knew
[C]  Were wrapped up in a royal bl[Am]ue

{comment: Verse 5}
I heard my friends laughing out a[F]cross the fields
The girls in the gloaming and the [C]birds on the wheel
The raw smell of horses and the wa[F]rm smell of hay
Cicadas electric in the heat [C]of the day
A run of Three Sisters and the flu[F]sh of the land
And the lake was a diamond in the v[C]alley’s hand
The straight of the highway and the [F]scattered out hearts
They were coming together they [C]pulling apart
And angels everywhere were in m[F]y midst
In the ones that I loved in the [C]ones that I kissed
I wondered what it was I’d b[F]een looking for up above
Heaven is so big there ain’t[C] no need to look up
So I stopped looking for royal c[F]ities in the air
Only a full house gonna ha[C]ve a prayer

{comment: Outro}
  [C]      [F]      [C]      [F]

title: Sonnys Dream (ver 2)
body:
For Tony Dwyer (1963-2018)

I cooked up these chords for my dad so he and I could play this down at the local 
bar & restaurant and play with friends of ours. I coudn't find a decent tab that was already online, so 
I decided to post mine. I hope you enjoy this classic Canadian Newfoundland tune!

{comment: Intro}
[G] 

{comment: Verse 1}
Sonny l[G]ives on a farm on a wide open space
Where you can take off your shoes and g[C]ive up the ra[G]ce
You could l[D]ay down your head by a sweet river bed
But Sonny a[C]lways remembers what it was his Mama s[G]aid[D]

{comment: Chorus}
Oh, Sonny d[G]on't go away, I am here all alone
Your daddy's a sailor who n[C]ever comes h[G]ome
All these n[D]ights get so long and the silence goes on
And I'm f[C]eeling so tired, I'm not all that st[G]rong[D]

{comment: Verse 2}
Sonny c[G]arries a load though he's barely a man
There ain't all that to do, still he d[C]oes what he c[G]an
And he w[D]atches the sea from a room by the stairs
And the w[C]aves keep on rollin', they've done that for y[G]ears[D]

{comment: Chorus}
Oh, Sonny d[G]on't go away, I am here all alone
Your daddy's a sailor who n[C]ever comes h[G]ome
All these n[D]ights get so long, the silence goes on
And I'm f[C]eeling so tired, I'm not all that st[G]rong

{comment: Instrumental}
  [D]      [D]      [C]      [C]  [G]    [D] 

{comment: Verse 3}
Sonny's d[G]reams can't be real, they're just stories he's read
They're just stars in his eyes, they're just d[C]reams in his h[G]ead
And he's h[D]ungry inside for the wide world outside
And I k[C]now I can't hold him though I've tried and I've t[G]ried[D]

{comment: Chorus}
Oh, Sonny d[G]on't go away, I am here all alone
Your daddy's a sailor who n[C]ever comes h[G]ome
All these n[D]ights get so long, the silence goes on
And I'm f[C]eeling so tired, I'm not all that st[G]rong[D]

artist: Buddy Wasisname and the Other Fellers
title: Saltwater Joys
body:
Band: Buddy Wasisname
Album: The Series Stuff
Tabbed by: Patrick Guiney
Email: pguiney_@hotmail.com
I suggest a soft easy strumming, better sound! If you know the song you know how great it is. Have fun!

{comment: Verse 1}
Just to [C]wake up in the [G]morning to the [Am]quiet of the [F]cove
And [C]hear aunt Bessie [G]talking to her[C]self.
And to [C]hear poor uncle [G]John mumbling [Am]wishes to old [F]Nell
It [C]made me feel like [G]everything was [C]fine.

{comment: Chorus 1}
I was [G]born down by the [Am]water, it's [F]here I'm gonna [C]stay
I've [G]searched for all the rea[Am]sons why [F]I should go awa[C]y.
but I haven't got the [G]thirst for all thos[Am]e modern-day [F]toys
So I'll just take my [C]chances with those [G]saltwater [C]joys.

{comment: Verse 2}
Follo[C]wing the little [G]brook, as it [Am]trickles to the [F]shore
In the [C]autumn when the [G]trees are flaming [C]red.
Kic[C]king leaves that fall a[G]round me, watching [Am]sunset paint the [F]hills
It's [C]all I'll ever [G]need to feel at [C]home.

{comment: Chorus 2}
This [G]island that we [Am]cling to has been [F]handed down with [C]pride
By [G]folks who fought to [Am]live here, taking [F]hardships all in [C]stride.
So I'll compliment her [G]beauty, hold [Am]on to my good[F]byes
And I'll [C]stay, take my chances with those [G]saltwater [C]joys.

{comment: Verse 3}
How can [C]I leave those [G]mornings, with the [Am]sunrise on the [F]cove
And the [C]gulls like flies sur[G]rounding Clayton's [C]Wharf.
Platter's Is[C]land wrapped in [G]rainbow, in the [Am]evening after [F]fog
The [C]ocean smells are [G]perfume to my [C]soul.

{comment: Chorus 3}
Some [G]go to where the [Am]buildings [F]reach to meet the [C]clouds
Where [G]warm and gentle [Am]people turn to s[F]warmin', faceless [C]crowds.
So I'll do without their [G]riches, [Am]glamour and the [F]noise
And I'll [C]stay, take my chances with those [G]saltwater [C]joys.
Some [G]go to where the [Am]buildings [F]reach to meet the [C]clouds
Where [G]warm and gentle [Am]people turn to [F]swarmin', faceless [C]crowds.
So I'll do without their [G]riches, [Am]glamour and the [F]noise
And I'll [C]stay, take my chances with those [G]saltwater [C]joys

title: Thirsty Boots
body:
Song:     Thirsty Boots
Artist:   Bob Dylan
Album:    The Bootleg Series Vol. 10: Another Self Portrait (1969–1971), 2013
Written:  Eric Andersen (with amendments by Dylan)
URL’s:    https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Thirsty_Boots
          https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/
          The_Bootleg_Series_Vol._10:_Another_Self_Portrait_%281969%E2%80%931971%29
Tempo:    Moderato
Style:    Folk & Blues
Tuning:   Standard

Chords:
          EADGBe
[G]                
[Bm]                
[Am]                
[D]                
[Em]                
[C]                

{comment: Intro:(acoustic Lead, Piano And Harmonica Over)}
 [G]   [Bm]   [Am]   [D]     

{comment: Verse 1}
You've [G]been long and [Bm]on the open road,
[Am]You’ve been sleepin&rs[D]quo; in the rain.
[G]From the dirty words [Em]and muddy cells,
[Am]Your clothes are smeared and [D]stained.
But [G]the dirty words and [Em]muddy cells,
[Am]Will soon be judged in[D]sane.
So [G]only stop and [C]rest yourself, 
[Am]’Til you ar[D]e off again.

{comment: Chorus 1}
And, [G]take off your [C]thirsty boots,
And [G]stay for a[C]while.
Your [G]feet are hot and [Em]weary, 
[Am]From a dusty [D]mile.
And [G]maybe I can [C]make you laugh,
[Am]And maybe I can [D]try.
[G]Just looking for [Em]the evening,
[C]And the mornin&r[D]squo; in[G] your eye.

{comment: Refrain:(acoustic Lead Over)}
 [G]   [Em]   [C]  [D]  

{comment: Verse 2}
Then [G]tell me of the [Bm]ones you saw,
[Am]As far as you could [D]see.
A[G]cross the plains from [Em]field to town, 
[Am]Marching to be [D]free.
And [G]of the rusty p[Bm]rison gates,
[Am]That tumble by [D]degree,
Like [G]laughing children, [C]one by one, 
[Am]Who look like you and [D]me.

{comment: Chorus 2}
Then, [G]take off your [C]thirsty boots,
And [G]stay for a[C]while.
Your [G]feet are hot and [Em]weary, 
[Am]From a dusty [D]mile.
And [G]maybe I can [C]make you laugh,
[Am]And maybe I can [D]try.
[G]Just looking for the [Em]evening,
[Am]And the mornin&r[D]squo; in[G] your eye.

{comment: Instrumental:(acoustic Lead, Piano And Harmonica Over)}
 [G]   [Em]   [Am]   [D]     

{comment: Verse 3}
I [G]know that you&rsq[Bm]uo;re no stranger,  
[Am]Down the crooked rainbow [D]trail.
From [G]dancing cliffheads, [Em]shattered sills,
[Am]Of slandered shackled [D]jails.
But [G]the voices drift up [Bm]from below, 
[Am]As his walls are being [D]scaled.
[G]And all of this, 
[Em]And more my friend, 
[Am]Your song shall not be [D]failed.

{comment: Chorus 3}
Then, [G]take off your [Em]thirsty boots,
And [Am]stay for a[D]while.
Your [G]feet are hot and [Em]weary, 
[Am]From a dusty [D]mile.
And [G]maybe I can [C]make you laugh,
[Am]And maybe I can [D]try.
[G]Just looking for the [Em]evening,
[Am]And the mornin&r[D]squo; in[G] your eye.

{comment: Coda:(acoustic Lead, Piano And Harmonica Over)}
 [G]   [Em]   [Am]   [D]     
 [G]           

(End)

1.  "Thirsty Boots" is a Civil Rights era folksong by American singer-songwriter Eric Andersen that first 
appeared on his 1966 album 'Bout Changes 'n' Things. According to the album's liner notes, the song "was 
written to a civil rights worker-friend. Having never gone down to Mississippi myself, I wrote the song 
about coming back."
2.  I’ve tried to make it more simply accessible, by changing it’s key to match the recorded version, by 
moving the capo to the fifth fret.  Hence, these chords could be easily played ‘open’ on a ukulele, which 
is in standard A tuning.  
3.  It has resonance, as this song could easily be about the suffering of present-day refugees and internee’s 
in our Australian ’detention centres’, both in and off-shore. :-R

title: Its All Over Now Baby Blue
body:
It's All Over Now, Baby Blue
Bob Dylan

Transcription by jwblair@ix.netcom.com

Bringing It All Back Home version
or capo all strings except for bottom E to create C drone

       CADGBe
[C]Cadd4  033010 (add the little finger on the 4th string)
G      220000 (actually an Em7, but this is what he plays for G)
[F]Dm     200231 (use the thumb to fret the 6th string)
E      x22100 (or maybe E7: x22130)

(Note: be sure to strum the 6th string to keep the distinctive
droning C note going.)

{comment: Intro}

[C](h[Cadd4]ammering onto the 4th string at the 2nd fret)

{comment: Verse 1}

[G]You must leave now, take what you need, you [F]think will [C]last
But what[G]ever you wish to keep, you [F]better [C]grab it fast
[Dm]Yonder stands your [F]orphan with his [C]gun
[Dm]Crying like a [F]fire in the [C]sun
[E7]Look out the saints are [F]coming [G]through
And [Dm]it's all over [F]now, Baby [C]Blue.

{comment: Verse 2}

[G]The highway is for gamblers, [F]better [C]use your sense.
[G]Take what you have gathered [F]from co[C]incidence.
The [Dm]empty-handed [F]painter from your [C]streets
Is [Dm]drawing crazy [F]patterns on your [C]sheets.
[E7]The sky, too, is folding [F]under [G]you
And [Dm]it's all over [F]now, Baby [C]Blue.

{comment: Verse 3}

[G]All your seasick sailors, [F]they are [C]rowing home.
Your [G]empty handed armies, [F]they're all [C]going home.
Your [Dm]lover who [F]just walked out the [C]door
Has [Dm]taken all his [F]blankets from the [C]floor.
The [E7]carpet, too, is moving[F] under [G]you
And [Dm]it's all over [F]now, Baby [C]Blue.

(Harmonica Solo)

[G]      [F]    [C] 
[G]      [F]    [C] 
[Dm]      [F]    [C] 
[Dm]      [F]    [C] 
[E7]      [F]    [G] 
[Dm]      [F]    [C] 

{comment: Verse 4}

Leave your [G]stepping stones behind, now [F]something that [C]calls for you.
For[G]get the dead you've left, they [F]will not [C]follow you.
The [Dm]vagabond who's [F]rapping at your [C]door
Is [Dm]standing in the [F]clothes that you once [C]wore.
[E7]Strike another match, go [F]start a[G]new
And [Dm]it's all over [F]now, Baby [C]Blue.

title: Dont Swallow The Cap
body:
Don’t Swallow the Cap
The National

The C/Bb chord is just to get a nice transition from Am, and it makes it sound 
more like the recording, you can also briefly play a Bb.

{comment: Intro}

[C/Bb]It&rsq[C]uo;s a sign that someone loves me
[Dm]I can hardly stand upright
[F]Hit my head upon the light
[Am]I have faith but don’t believe it
[C/Bb]It's no[C]t there enough to leave it

[C/Bb]Cause [C]I was not supposed to be here.

body:

In London [G7]city where I did [C]dwell
A butcher [G7]boy, I loved right [C]well
He courted [Am]me, my life aw[Dm]ay
But now with [G7]me, he will not [C]stay

{comment: Chorus}
I wish, I [G7]wish, I wish in [C]vain
I wish I [G7]was a maid ag[C]ain
A maid [Am]again I ne'er will [Dm]be
'Till cherries [G7]grow on an ivy [C]tree

I wish my [G7]baby it was [C]born
And smiling [G7]on its daddy's [C]knee
And me poor [Am]girl to be dead and [Dm]gone
With the long green [G7]grass growing over [C]me

She went ups[G7]tairs to go to [C]bed
And calling [G7]to her mother [C]said
"Give me a c[Am]hair 'till I sit [Dm]down
And a pen and [G7]ink 'till I write [C]down"

At every [G7]word she dropped a [C]tear
And at every [G7]line cried "Willie [C]dear -
Oh, what a [Am]foolish girl was [Dm]I
To be led as[G7]tray by a butcher [C]boy"

He went up[G7]stairs and the door he [C]broke
He found her [G7]hanging from a [C]rope
He took his k[Am]nife and he cut her [Dm]down
And in her [G7]pocket, these words he [C]found

Oh, make my [G7]grave large, wide and [C]deep
Put a marble [G7]stone at my head and [C]feet
And in the [Am]middle, a turtle [Dm]dove
That the world may [G7]know, that I died for [C]love.

title: Silver Dagger
body:
Artist: Bob Dylan and Joan Baez
Album: The Bootleg Series Vol. 6: Bob Dylan Live 1964
Author: Traditional/arr. Joan Baez

D[G]on't sing l[D]ove songs; you'll wake my m[G]other,

She's sleepi[D]ng here, right by m[Em]y side.

And in her r[C]ight hand, a silver d[D]agger.

She s[G]ays that I [C]can't be your bri[D]de.

All men are false, says my mother,
They'll tell you wicked, lovin' lies.
The very next evening, they'll court another,
Leave you alone to pine and sigh.

My daddy is a handsome devil
He's got a chain five miles long,
And on every link a heart does dangle
Of another maid he's loved and wronged.

Go court another tender maiden,
And hope that she will be your wife,
For I've been warned, and I've decided
To sleep alone all of my life.

title: Grey Foggy Day
body:
Great traditional Newfoundland song in 6/8 time.

{comment: Chorus Between Each Verse.}

[G]   [D]   [G] 

It's been some [C]years ago
Since I left from my [G]island
To go to the [D]mainland,
Like the old folks would [G]say;[C][G]
As I walked up the [C]gangway
And I stood on the [G]starboard
And I gazed on the [D]harbour
On a grey foggy [G]day.[C][G]

(Chorus)
Summer days they were [C]warmer then
When we laughed with the [G]old fishermen,
And they cursed when the [D]fog rolled in,
Then they made up the [G]hay;[C][G]
It's been more than a [C]long, long time
Since I held you and c[G]alled you mine,
And we waited for the [D]sun to shine
On a grey foggy [G]day.[C][G]

To wake in the [C]early morn
To the sound of the [G]old fog horn,
And wait for the [D]men to return
With their boats in the [G]bay;[C][G]
All these things I don't [C]see no more
When I lived on the [G]old cape shore,
And I gazed on the [D]boats on the moors
On a grey foggy [G]day.[C][G]

Summer days they were [C]warmer then
When we laughed with the [G]old fishermen,
And they cursed when the [D]fog rolled in,
Then they made up the [G]hay;[C][G]
It's been more than a [C]long, long time
Since I held you and c[G]alled you mine,
And we waited for the s[D]un to shine
On a grey foggy [G]day.[C][G]

And as long as my [C]heart don't break,
From those old memo[G]ries,
Old lovers and o[D]ld used-to-be's,
I'll come home to s[G]tay;[C][G]
I can still hear the [C]ocean roar
Through the hills on the o[G]ld cape shore,
There are no fishing [D]boats anymore,
But it's a grey foggy [G]day.[C][G]

Summer days they were [C]warmer then
When we laughed with the [G]old fishermen,
And they cursed when the [D]fog rolled in,
Then they made up the [G]hay;[C][G]
It's been more than a [C]long, long time
Since I held you and [G]called you mine,
And we waited for the [D]sun to shine
On a grey foggy [G]day.[C][G]

And I pray that the [D]sun will shine ....
On this grey foggy [G]day.[C][G]

title: If We Make It Through December
body:
To sound closer to Phoebe's piano version, I recommend alternating your thumb playing the root note and your
other fingers plucking the other strings! In the recording they sometimes add in some descending bass notes
between chord changes, but for the most part you can get away with just playing the chords through it.

Anywhere you see a B*, slide your index finger to the first fret but keep the other fingers the same!

title: Cleopatra
body:
Artist: The Lumineers

Transcription: SCA, kostya.levin.sca@gmail.com 

VERSION 1
Tuning: standard

Chords used:

{comment: Intro}
[G] 

{comment: Verse}
[G]I was Cleopatra, I was young and an actress
When you knelt by my mattress, and asked for my [D]hand
But I was [G]sad you asked it, as I laid in a black dress
With my father in a casket, I had no [D]plans

(Break)
[G] 

{comment: Pre-chorus}

And it hardened like my heart did when you left [D]town
But I [G]must admit it, that I would [C]marry you in an i[G]nstant
Damn your wife, I'd be your mistress just to have you [D]around

{comment: Chorus}
But I was [G]late for [D]this, [C]late for [G]that, [C]late for the [G]love of my [D]life

(Break)
[G] 

{comment: Pre-chorus Ii}
While the [G]church discouraged, any [C]lust that burned within [G]me
Yes my flesh, it was my currency, but I held [D]true
So I [G]drive a taxi, and the [C]traffic [G]distracts me
From the [C]strangers in my [G]backseat, they remind me [D]of you

{comment: Chorus}
But I was [G]late for [D]this, [C]late for [G]that, [C]late for the [G]love of my [D]life
And when I [G]die [D]alone, when I [C]die [G]alone, when I [C]die I'll [G]be on [D]time

{comment: Bridge}
And the [Em]only gifts from my [G]Lord were a [C]birth [D]and a [Em]divorce
But I've [G]read this script and the [Em]costume fits, [C]so I'll p[D]lay my [G]part

(Break)
[G] 

{comment: Pre-chorus Ii}
I was [G]Cleopatra, I was [C]taller than the [G]rafters
But that's all in the past now, gone with the [D]wind
Now a [G]nurse in white shoes [C]leads me back to my [G]guestroom
It's a [C]bed and a [G]bathroom
And a place for the [D]end

{comment: Chorus}

And when I [G]die [D]alone, when I [C]die [G]alone, when I [C]die I'll [G]be on [D]time

title: Annies Going To Sing Her Song
body:
---------------------------------------------------------------------------

Tuning: Standard

title: Señor Tales Of Yankee Power
body:
This is the sixth song off of Dylan's underrated -Street Legal- album.  I
happen to love the song (and some of the album, itself) to death.  Anyway,
what one should remember about this is that there are a lot of bass walk-
downs in this song, and they are slow, as is the song.  What I mean, for
example, is that, say the song moves from C to Am, the bass goes C, B, A.

{comment: Verse 1}
[Am]Señor,
[Em]Señor,
Can you [F]tell me where we [C]heading?
Lincoln County Road or Armaged[Am]don?
Seems like I been [G]down this way be[F]fore
[Dm]Is there any truth in that, [Am]Señor?

{comment: Verse 2}
[Am]Señor,
[Em]Señor,
Do you [F]know where she's [C]hiding?
How long are we gonna be [Am]riding?
How long must I keep my [G]eyes glued to the [F]door?
[Dm]Will there be any comfort here, [Am]Señor?

{comment: Verse 3}
There's a [C]wicked wind still blowing on that [Em]upper deck
There's an [F]iron cross still hanging down from [Am]around her neck
There's a [C]marching band still playing in their [Em]vacant lot
Where she [F]held me in her arms one time and said "[Am]forget what we got"

{comment: Verse 4}
[Am]Señor,
[Em]Señor,
I can [F]see the painted [C]wagon
Smell the tail of a [Am]dragon
Can't stand the [G]suspense any[F]more
Can you [Dm]tell me who to contact here, [Am]Señor?

(Instrumental) (all of the verse chords with the cool mandolin)
[Am]   [Em]   [F]  [C]  [Am]   [G]  [F]  [Dm]   [Am]  

{comment: Verse 5}
Well the [C]last thing I remember before they [Em]stripped and kneeled
Was a [F]train load of fools bogged down in a [Am]Magnetic field
The [C]gypsy, with his broken flag and a [Em]flashing ring
He say, "[F]Son this ain't a dream no more, it's the [Am]real thing"

{comment: Verse 6}
[Am]Señor,
[Em]Señor,
You know their [F]hearts here are hard as [C]leather
Well give me a minute, let me [Am]get it together
Just gotta [G]pick myself up off the [F]floor
[Dm]I'm ready when you are, [Am]Señor?

Another Instrumental like the first Instrumental

{comment: Verse 7}
[Am]Señor,
[Em]Señor,
Let's [F]overturn these [C]tables
Disconnect these [Am]cables
This place don't make [G]sense to me no m[F]ore
Can you [Dm]tell me what we're waiting for, [Am]Señor?

The song fades out and ends on the verse chords and that is about it.
The Am* chords are played by striking the Am chord, then you release all your
fingers so that you strike all open strings (except for the low E and A
strings), then you strike back on the Am chord.  It may not be accurate but
that is how I make sense out of that part of the song.

title: Thrasher
body:
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=HOgSTD7Kjrw
Sounds in D, so: this version with Capo on 2.

{comment: Verse 1}

[C]They were hiding behind hay bales,
They were [F]planting in the [C]Full Moon,
They had given [C/B]all they [Am7]had for something [F]new,[G]
But the [C]light of day was on them,
They could [F]see the thrasher [C]coming,
And the [C]water [C/B]shone like [Am7]diamonds in the [F]dew.[G]

[F]And I was just [G]getting up,
To [C]hit the road before its [F]light,
trying to catch an [G]hour on the [C]sun,[Cmaj7][Cmaj7][C]
[F]When I saw those thrasher[G]s rolling by,
Looking [C]more than  [C/B]two lanes [Am7]wide,[Am/C]
I was [Dm7]feeling like my day had just [G]begun.

{comment: Verse 2}

Where the [C]eagles glide descending,
There's an [F]ancient river [C]bending,
Down the [C]timeless [C/B]gorge of [Am7]changes [Am7/G]where s[F]leeplessness a[G]waits,
I [C]searched out my companions,
who were [F]lost in crystal [C]canyons,
When the [C]aimless [C/B]blade of [Am7]science [Am7/G]slashed the [F]pearly gates,[G]

[F]It was then I knew I'd [G]had enough, burned my [C]credit card for [F]fuel,
[F]Headed out to where the [G]pavement turns to [C]sand,[Cmaj7][C]
With a [F]one-way ticket to the [G]land of truth and my [C]suitcase [C/B]in my [Am7]hand.[Am7/G]
How I [Dm7]lost my friends I still dont understan[G]d.

{comment: Verse 3}

[C]They had the best selection,
They were [F]poisoned with pro[C]tection,
There was [C]nothing [C/B]that they [Am7]needed, [Am7/G]
[F]nothing left to [G]find.
They were [C]lost in rock formations,
Or [F]became park bench mut[C]ations,
On the the [C]sidewalks [C/B]and in the [Am7]stations,[Am7/G]      they were [F]waiting, [G]waiting.

[F]So I got bored and [G]left them there,
They were [C]just dead weight to [F]me,
[F]Better down the [G]road without that [C]load,[Cmaj7][Cmaj7][C]
[F]Bring's back the time I was [G]eight or nine,
I was [C]watching my Mama's [Am7]TV,[Am7/G]
It was that [Dm7]great Grand Canyon rescue episod[G]e.
With the [C]vulture glides descending,
On an [F]asphalt highway [C]bending,
Through [C]libraries [C/B]and muse[Am7]ums,[Am7/G]       [F]galaxies and [G]stars.

{comment: Verse 4}

Down the [C]windy halls of friendship,
To the [F]rose clipped by the [C]bullwhip,
The [C]motel of [C/B]lost c[Am7]ompanions [Am7/G]waits with [F]heated pool and [G]bar.
[F]But me, I'm not [G]stopping there,
Got my o[C]wn row left to [F]hoe,
[F]Just another [G]line in the field of [C]time.[Cmaj7]

[F]When the thrashers come I'll be [G]stuck in the sun,
Like the [C]dinosaurs in [Am7]shrines,[Am7/G]
But I'll [Dm7]know the time has come to give what's [G]mine.

title: Hallelujah
body:
{comment: Verse 1}
Now I've [G]heard there was a se[Em]cret chord
That [G]David played, and it pl[Em]eased the Lord
But [C]you don't really [D]care for music, do y[G]ou?[D]
It [G]goes like this the [C]fourth, the [D]fifth
The [Em]minor fall, the [C]major lift
The [D]baffled king com[B7]posing Halle[Em]lujah

{comment: Verse 2}

And [D]from your lips she [B7]drew the Halle[Em]lujah

{comment: Verse 3}
You [G]say I took the [Em]name in vain
[G]I don't even [Em]know the name
But [C]if I did, well [D]really, what's it [G]to ya?[D]
There's a [G]blaze of light in [C]every [D]word
It [Em]doesn't matter [C]which you heard
The [D]holy or the [B7]broken Halle[Em]lujah
Halle[C]lujah, Halle[Em]lujah, Halle[C]lujah, Halle[G]lu-[D]u-[G]jah[D]

title: Alexandra Leaving (ver 4)
body:
#This version is as performed by the songs co-writer Sharon Robinson, during Cohen's final tour. Capo on 1st

[G]Suddenly the night has grown c[Em]older.[D]
The Go[D]d of love pr[C]eparing [D]to d[G]epart.
[G]Alexandra hoisted on his s[Em]houlder,[D]
T[D]hey slip between the s[C]entries [D]of the h[G]eart.

[Bm]Upheld by the simplicities of [C]pleasure,
[Am]They gain the light, they formlessly en[D]twine;[Dsus4][D]
[G]And radiant beyond your widest [Em]measure
[Am]They fall among the v[C]oices [D]and the [G]wine.

It's not a t[C]rick, your se[D]nses all d[Em]eceiving,
A fitful dr[C]eam, the mo[Am]rning will ex[D]haust[Dsus4][D]

title: Every Grain Of Sand (ver 3)
body:
Every Grain Of Sand chords  acoustic 
Bob Dylan 1981 

https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Every_Grain_of_Sand

[D]  [G]    

In the [D]time of my con[G]fession, in the [D]hour of my deepest [G]need
When the [D]pool of tears be[G]neath my feet [A]flood every newborn seed
There's a [D]dying voice with[G]in me [D]reaching out some[G]where
[D]Toiling in the [G]danger and in the [A]morals of despair.

Don't [A]have the inclination to look [D]back on any mis[A]take
Like Cain, I now behold this chain of e[D]vents that I must [A]break
In the [D]fury of the [G]moment I can [D]see the master's [G]hand
In [D]every leaf that [G]trembles, in [A]every [G]grain of [D]sand.

Oh, the [D]flowers of indul[G]gence and the [D]weeds of yester[G]year
Like [D]criminals, they have [G]choked the breath of [A]conscience and good cheer
The [D]sun beat down u[G]pon the steps of [D]time to light the [G]way 
To [D]ease the pain of [G]idleness and the [A]memory of decay.

I [A]gaze into the doorway of [D]temptation's angry [A]flame
And every time I pass that way I [D]always hear my [A]name
Then [D]onward in my [G]journey I [D]come to under[G]stand
That [D]every hair is [G]numbered like [A]every [G]grain of [D]sand.

break

I have [D]gone from rags to [G]riches in the [D]sorrow of the [G]night
In the [D]violence of a [G]summer's dream, in the [A]chill of a wintry light
In the [D]bitter dance of [G]loneliness [D]fading into [G]space
In the [D]broken mirror of [G]innocence on [A]each forgotten face.

I [A]hear the ancient footsteps like the [D]motion of the [A]sea
Sometimes I turn, there's someone there, other [D]times it's only [A]me
I am [D]hanging in the [G]balance of the [D]reality of [G]man
Like [D]every sparrow [G]falling, like [A]every [G]grain of [D]sand.
